title: polypodium vulgare subsp. columbianum
intro: Polypodium vulgare subsp. columbianum, also known as Western polypody, is a subspecies of the Polypodium vulgare fern, native to the western coast of North America, particularly in the Pacific Northwest region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Western polypody include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic to aid in wound healing. Western polypody can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, teas, or tinctures, but caution is advised due to potential interactions with medications or allergic reactions.
